title: xanthoxalis phaeotricha
intro: Xanthoxalis phaeotricha, also known as Yellow Wood Sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, known for its yellow flowers and edible leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves of Xanthoxalis phaeotricha contain oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving digestion.
